name: Valdecañas Reservoir Loop
name_original: Belvís de Monroy y Almaraz desde Bohonal de Ibor – circular por el río Tajo
description: This 33.3-mile moderate road cycling route near Bohonal de Ibor offers historical sites, Tajo River views, and diverse Extremadura landscape
region_intro: Road cycling around Bohonal De Ibor is characterized by diverse landscapes within the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark, offering varied terrain from flat roads to challenging mountain climbs. The region features the Tajo, Gualija, and Ibor rivers, alongside the expansive Valdecañas Reservoir, providing scenic routes. Cyclists encounter Mediterranean forests with holm and cork oaks, as well as distinctive rock formations known as "canchos." The area's geology contributes to a mix of elevations, including significant ascents. Best road cycling routes around Bohonal De Ibor The most popular road cycling route is Villuercas Geopark: Arrebatacapas and Hospital Pass Loop, a 69.5 miles (111.9 km) trail that takes 5 hours 32 minutes to complete. This difficult route traverses the Geopark's mountain scenery with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Valdecañas Reservoir Loop, a moderate 33.3 miles (53.5 km) path. This route offers scenic views around the large body of water, often on paved roads. Local road cyclists also love the Los Ibores Loop, a 42.0 miles (67.6 km) trail leading through the Ibor River valleys and Mediterranean forests, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Road cycling around Bohonal De Ibor is defined by the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark, the Valdecañas Reservoir, and the Ibor River valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult mountain ascents. The routes in Bohonal De Ibor are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Bohonal De Ibor's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Bohonal de Ibor?
A: Road cycling around Bohonal de Ibor offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find everything from flat, paved roads, especially around the Valdecañas Reservoir, to challenging climbs in the mountains of the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark. The landscape features Mediterranean forests with holm and cork oaks, river valleys, and distinctive rock formations known as "canchos."

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Bohonal de Ibor?
A: Yes, Bohonal de Ibor offers routes suitable for various skill levels. There are 2 easy and 5 moderate road cycling routes available. For a scenic, moderate ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058794665/valdecanas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 53.5 km with manageable elevation.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes in the area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Bohonal de Ibor has 4 difficult routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371640528/villuercas-geopark-arrebatacapas-and-hospital-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villuercas Geopark: Arrebatacapas and Hospital Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 111.9 km route with significant elevation gain through the Geopark's mountain scenery. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371363079/los-ibores-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Ibores Loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering 67.6 km through river valleys and forests.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Bohonal de Ibor?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Bohonal de Ibor are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058794665/valdecanas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371370213/belvis-de-monroy-y-portico-de-curia-de-talavera-la-vieja-desde-bohonal-de-ibor-circular-por-alto-de-las-mesas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belvís de Monroy y Pórtico de Curia de Talavera la Vieja desde Bohonal de Ibor— circular por Alto de las Mesas&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I see while cycling in Bohonal de Ibor?
A: The region is rich in history. You can cycle past the reconstructed Roman &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692387" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Temple of the Woodcutters of Augustobriga&lt;/a&gt;, which was moved from the submerged city of Augustóbriga. Other notable sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5583007" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belvís de Monroy&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5692454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of San Bartolomé Apóstol&lt;/a&gt; in Bohonal de Ibor itself.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on road cycling routes in this area?
A: Bohonal de Ibor is situated within the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark, offering stunning natural beauty. You'll ride alongside the Tajo, Gualija, and Ibor rivers, and enjoy views of the expansive Valdecañas Reservoir. The routes often traverse Mediterranean forests with holm and cork oaks, and you might spot unique rock formations known as "canchos."

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Bohonal de Ibor?
A: The road cycling routes in Bohonal de Ibor are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 5.0 stars. Over 500 road cyclists have explored the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from reservoir views to challenging mountain ascents within the Geopark.

Q: Is Bohonal de Ibor a good destination for road cycling with a family?
A: While some routes offer challenging climbs, there are also easier and moderate options that could be suitable for families with varying experience levels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058794665/valdecanas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a moderate route with scenic views that might appeal to families looking for a less strenuous ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Bohonal de Ibor?
A: The region's climate suggests that spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for road cycling, offering milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be very hot, while winters are cooler but often still suitable for cycling, especially on sunny days.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Valdecañas Reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058794665/valdecanas-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdecañas Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase the scenic views around this large body of water. Many other routes in the vicinity will also offer glimpses or extended sections along the reservoir's shores.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Bohonal de Ibor?
A: There are 11 road cycling routes documented around Bohonal de Ibor, offering a range of distances and difficulties to suit different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark?
A: Yes, many routes are specifically designed to traverse the unique landscapes of the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1371640528/villuercas-geopark-arrebatacapas-and-hospital-pass-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villuercas Geopark: Arrebatacapas and Hospital Pass Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, taking you through the Geopark's stunning mountain scenery.
tips: Remains of a marble temple from an ancient Roman city called Augustobriga. Spectacular views of the Valdecañas reservoir and the bridge that crosses Guadalupe.

Belvís de Monroy is a small town in the Campo Arañuelo region, next to the Tagus River, near the entrance to the Monfragüe National Park. The medieval castle of Belvís el Monroy dominates the town dominating the surrounding landscape and can be seen from afar.
Crossing the Tagus River from Valdemoreno, you begin to climb by bicycle to see the dam of the Valdecañas Reservoir.
Bohonal de Ibor is a small town near the Valdecañas reservoir and the Tagus river. Stop at Plaza España to eat something and have a coffee and admire the Church of San Bartolomé Apóstol.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route begins and ends in Bohonal de Ibor. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the village itself to access the trailhead conveniently.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for road cycling and generally takes around 2 hours and 48 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly well-paved and easily passable, making it suitable for road bikes. You'll encounter narrow roads surrounded by crops, and while generally smooth, be aware that some sections of the broader Tagus Cycle Route in the area can include varied surfaces or dirt trails. This specific circular route, however, is expected to be mostly paved.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a mix of historical sites and natural beauty. Notable points include the Temple of the Woodcutters of Augustobriga, the Castle of Belvís de Monroy, and the Church of San Bartolomé Apóstol, Bohonal de Ibor. You'll also enjoy impressive panoramic views of the Valdecañas Reservoir, especially from the View of Valdecañas Reservoir Dam.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and an 'average road bike ride'. It covers approximately 53.5 kilometers with about 700 meters of elevation gain, so it requires good physical condition. While not extremely difficult, beginners might find it challenging due to its length and elevation.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences Mediterranean climate. Spring (March to May) and Autumn (September to November) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential chill of winter. These seasons also showcase the local flora at its best.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route shares sections with several official trails, including the Camino Natural del Tajo (31% of the route), Camino de Monfragüe - Etapa 5 (20%), and Camino de Monfragüe - Etapa 4 (9%). You'll also briefly encounter parts of the Camino de los Jerónimos and Camino del Canal.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No specific cycling permits are generally required to ride this route. It passes through public areas and roads. However, always respect private property and local regulations.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily a road cycling path, bringing dogs along on a bike can be challenging. If you plan to cycle with a dog, ensure they are safely secured in a trailer or basket designed for cycling, and always keep them on a leash when off the bike, especially in populated areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: What kind of natural scenery and wildlife can I expect?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through diverse landscapes featuring Mediterranean flora like olive groves, almond trees, and holm oaks. The route offers impressive panoramic views of the Valdecañas Reservoir and is part of the Villuercas-Ibores-Jara Geopark, highlighting its geological heritage. Keep an eye out for local birdlife and other small animals typical of Extremadura's rural areas.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near villages like Bohonal de Ibor, Belvís de Monroy, and Almaraz. These towns are your best bet for finding cafes, restaurants, or shops to refuel and refresh during your ride. It's always a good idea to check opening hours in advance, especially in smaller communities.
